L1.area: DOM document model and serialization infrastructure

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where a class name is read from an untrusted input stream and used to instantiate an arbitrary object via reflection without verifying that the class is of the expected type. When the standard static factory method is not found on the deserialized class, the code falls back to calling the no-argument constructor on the attacker-controlled class, allowing instantiation of arbitrary classes and potentially leading to remote code execution.

L3.area: DOM document serialization and DOMImplementation restoration logic
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in the DOM document deserialization path where a class name is read from an untrusted serialization stream and used to instantiate an object via reflection without verifying that the resulting class implements the expected DOM interface. When the static factory method lookup fails, the code unconditionally falls back to calling the no-argument constructor on the attacker-supplied class. An attacker can craft a malicious serialized document to trigger arbitrary class instantiation, potentially leading to remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The readObject deserialization method reads a class name from the input stream and instantiates it via reflection without checking whether the class implements DOMImplementation, allowing an attacker to instantiate arbitrary classes via a crafted serialized object.
